repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: All five selected memories (ca6k_00045, ca6k_00227, ca6k_00322, ca6k_00285, ca6k_00390) share the exact root cause pattern as the current failure: redefinition of class ge::op::XXXCustom due to duplicate REG_OP macro in the generated op_proto.h. The current query shows 'redefinition of class ge::op::Ca6k02116Custom' and a DuplicateSectionError in the ops-info.ini, both indicating duplicate operator registration. These memories provide concrete fixes: removing manually added REG_OP or OP_ADD blocks when autogen already generates them, ensuring only one registration per operator. The other manifest entries (missing blocks, TilingFunc errors, vector exceptions) are unrelated to this build-stage duplicate registration error.
